human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, authority-source baseline, scenario suite, evaluation results, readiness review, deployment notes, and marketing notes for consistency with issue #136 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CRM administration, configuration, supported native automation, supported import/export and sync guidance, lifecycle readiness, troubleshooting, and platform governance.
Real public authoritative sources are documented with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy in the manifest.

The package includes CRM-specific scenarios for role and record access, native workflow quality, imports, integration constraints, release readiness, governance, edge cases, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
The manifest defines bootstrap metadata, delegated request and response contracts, and fallback behavior for shared meta-agents required by issue #136.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local CRM source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment packaging normalization, and commercialization wording review are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffold consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the specialist-owned CRM boundary.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #136.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation and adaptation constraints, rollback path, monitoring and logging expectations, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without widening scope.
